description: Milestone 02 box CRUD: add stable object creation, exact-frame box overlays, and persisted manual box create/edit/delete flows tied to backend-canonical frame indices.

userStories.title: Persist object tracks and frame annotations for manual boxes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want stored object-track and frame-annotation records so that manual box work survives reloads and stays tied to explicit frame indices.
acceptanceCriteria: Add persisted models for object tracks and frame annotations linked to indexed videos, with stable object identity and one annotation row per `(video_id, frame_idx, object_id)`
Frame annotation storage keeps canonical `frame_idx`, `object_id`, `is_keyframe`, `source`, and normalized `box_x`, `box_y`, `box_w`, `box_h` fields for manual boxes
Document the stored milestone-02 object and frame-annotation fields in `docs/engineering/data-model.md`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add video manifest read path and object creation endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to load object summaries and create a new object so that I can pick a stable target before drawing boxes.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `GET /api/videos/{video_id}/manifest` to return selected video metadata, persisted object summaries, annotated frame indices, and keyframe indices
Implement `POST /api/videos/{video_id}/objects` to create a persisted object for the video and return its stable object id plus object summary fields used by the UI
A newly created object appears in a later manifest reload for the same video
Document the manifest and object-create contracts in `docs/engineering/api.md`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement backend frame annotation CRUD endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want exact-frame annotations saved and loaded through backend APIs so that manual boxes persist independently from browser playback time.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `GET /api/videos/{video_id}/annotations`, `GET /api/videos/{video_id}/annotations/frame/{frame_idx}`, `PUT /api/videos/{video_id}/annotations/frame/{frame_idx}`, and `DELETE /api/videos/{video_id}/annotations/frame/{frame_idx}/object/{object_id}`
Request and response parsing validates canonical `frame_idx` handling and normalized four-value `box_xywh_norm` payloads at the API boundary
Backend integration tests cover create, load, update, and delete for one object's annotation on one exact frame, including reload from a clean app instance
Document frame-annotation CRUD contracts and frame-scoped persistence rules in `docs/engineering/api.md` and `docs/engineering/architecture.md`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add typed frontend annotation workspace module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a feature-scoped frontend data and state module for objects and frame annotations so that box UI stays typed and keeps backend frame index as the source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Add typed frontend client methods for manifest, object creation, frame-annotation load, frame-annotation upsert, and frame-annotation delete endpoints
Parse backend JSON payloads at the frontend boundary before object or annotation data enters UI state
Feature state keeps selected object, loaded frame annotations, and draft box-edit data separate from canonical `currentFrameIndex` state
Add frontend tests for payload parsing and annotation workspace state transitions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build object list and object creation UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see available objects and create a new one so that I can choose what the current frame box belongs to.
acceptanceCriteria: Render an object list for the selected video using backend manifest data
User can create a new object from the review workspace and the UI selects or exposes that stable object id for later box actions
Reloading the page and reopening the same video shows the persisted object list from the backend
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser

userStories.title: Render exact-frame box overlay from persisted annotations
userStories.description: As a user, I want saved boxes drawn on the exact-frame pane so that I can inspect annotations on the backend-canonical image.
acceptanceCriteria: Changing or reopening canonical frame N loads that frame's persisted annotations and renders them as overlay boxes on the exact-frame pane
Overlay geometry maps normalized `xywh` values to the displayed exact-frame image, not to browser playback state
Overlay only shows annotations for the explicit selected frame index and updates when frame selection changes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser

userStories.title: Add draw-box creation flow on exact frames
userStories.description: As a user, I want to draw a box for the selected object on frame N so that I can create a manual annotation on the canonical image.
acceptanceCriteria: With a selected object and loaded exact frame, user can drag to draw a new box on frame N in the annotation pane
Saving the new box writes `object_id`, canonical `frame_idx`, `is_keyframe = true`, `source = manual`, and normalized `box_xywh_norm` through the backend annotation upsert path
Reloading the page and reopening the same video and frame shows the saved box from persisted backend data
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser

userStories.title: Add move and resize flow for saved boxes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to move and resize an existing box so that I can correct its placement on the exact frame.
acceptanceCriteria: User can drag an existing box and resize it from overlay handles on the exact-frame pane
Saving an edit persists updated normalized `box_xywh_norm` for the same `video_id`, `frame_idx`, and `object_id`
Reloading the same frame shows edited box geometry from the backend instead of stale local UI state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser

userStories.title: Add current-frame box deletion flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete one box on the current exact frame so that I can remove a mistaken manual annotation without affecting other frames.
acceptanceCriteria: User can delete one object's annotation on the current exact frame through the backend frame-annotation delete path
After delete, the box disappears from the overlay and does not return when the same frame is reloaded
Deleting one frame annotation does not delete unrelated objects or annotations on other frames
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser
